topic: Gem Team adoption matrix: triage mubaidr/gem-team (external/gem-team-main) mechanisms for ADOPT/ADAPT/REJECT into the spec-kit, with file:line evidence both sides + an explicit already-have dedup pass. Produce research.md (verdict matrix) + sub-packet-proposals.md (candidate 027 child phases). Research-only; XCE corpus exhausted so this is net-new external signal.

noveltyJustification: Gem Team’s orchestration is mostly already covered; the only useful novelty is a clearer, compact routing/wave-integration presentation.
Gem Team contributes a clearer operational-hazard taxonomy and skill-candidate thresholding, but most memory mechanics are already stronger in Spec Kit.
Gem Team adds a concrete per-dispatch shared context snapshot pattern; Spec Kit already has stronger durable continuity but less typed, per-agent snapshot delivery.
Gem Team adds a useful explicit precedence contract, but most knowledge-layer artifacts already exist in spec-kit under stronger spec-folder, skill, and memory contracts.

Most value is additive tightening around source-verified claims, explicit OWASP/secrets/PII scanning, contract-first ordering, and a critic-style plan challenge gate; core verification and independent review already exist.
Gem Team adds materially new machine-checked handoff enforcement around an existing spec-kit diagnostic discipline.

Gem Team’s novel contribution is automatic confidence-thresholded pattern-to-skill extraction; spec-kit already has stronger scaffolding and evaluator-gated promotion but lacks this auto-discovery trigger.
Gem Team offers a clearer explicit routing matrix, but most phase-skipping behavior already exists in spec-kit as distributed level, gate, and agent fast-path rules.
Gem Team adds one useful packaging idea, a progressive context envelope, but most token-efficiency mechanisms already exist in spec-kit in more specific and measurable forms.
Gem’s explicit pre-mortem plus typed retry/action table is meaningfully new, but much of the recovery intent overlaps with spec-kit halt/escalation and deep-loop stuck-recovery machinery.

Gem Team’s strongest new contribution is not structured output itself, which spec-kit partly has, but a uniform typed envelope and per-agent input schemas that make orchestration decisions machine-parseable.

The changelog’s strongest new value is not new feature inventory, but reversal signal: classification and per-task parallel flags were tried and simplified away, while focus-scoped research lookup and accessibility-snapshot reliability remain concrete gaps.
Gem Team contributes a genuinely new packaging/deploy model, but only the single-source drift-control idea fits spec-kit’s current in-repo architecture.
Both claims survive only as narrower enforcement upgrades; the behavioral discipline already exists, while ordering/schema machine checks are the real residual gaps.

focus: RQ-V2 adversarial — multi-agent context (context-package + memory taxonomy)
noveltyJustification: The main new information is that `session_resume` already has a shared payload envelope and OpenCode transport plan, further shrinking Claim 1 beyond the earlier @context-only counterargument.
The only strong novelty left is mandatory pre-execution pre-mortem; typed resilience is already substantially covered, just fragmented.

focus: RQ-V4 adversarial — learning & knowledge (auto-skills + knowledge precedence)
noveltyJustification: Both claims survive only in narrowed form; the strongest novelty is auto-discovery from run learnings, while precedence mostly consolidates rules spec-kit already has.

focus: RQ-V5 adversarial — security gates + typed I/O contract
noveltyJustification: The main new information is that both claims are narrower than proposed: security coverage is already broad, and agent I/O is already structured but not uniformly typed.
The main new information is that specialized leaf-agent playbooks, PRD write-back semantics, and planner reviewer-focus scoring were under-carried even after the adversarial narrowing.

verdict: validate-and-incrementally-refine; 3 sub-packets (P1 typed-io-adapter, P2 scoped-gates, P3 planner-focus)

noveltyJustification: Only 2 of 20 sub-features (simplification-mode checklist, a11y audit schedule) are genuine gaps; the remaining 18 are either already covered or outside spec-kit scope by design.
The structured planner-emitted drift signal is the only genuinely absent mechanism; spec-kit's multi-layer contradiction handling and completion gates cover the remaining intent.

focus: RQ-M3 planner intelligence (quality_score / reviewer_focus / risk / conflict)
noveltyJustification: Two sub-features (quality_score plan self-assessment, reviewer_focus attention routing) are net-new gaps worth ADAPT-ing; the rest are covered or redundant.
One genuine gap (plan-template caching) and one partial gap (failure-context generalization); the other three signals are already covered or out of scope.

The three sub-packets are correctly scoped and genuinely net-new, but the gaps are smaller than gpt-5.5 characterized — particularly P1's output-side where @code RETURN already has typed enums and structured fields. The progressive-enrichment vs. snapshot architecture gap is the most significant missed finding.

result: cross-model corroboration of validate-don't-transform; P1 re-scoped to dispatch-input primary; P2 reframed as downscale; OWASP-coverage claim flagged
